contaminant.name: Electroless Nickel Plating
contaminant.description: Nickel-plating contamination, it manifests as thin metallic deposits from electrochemical processes, where nickel ions adhere tenaciously to substrates during plating operations gone awry. This layer, distinct from oxide scales or organic residues by its uniform conductivity and corrosion resistance, behaves differently across metals: on steel, it bonds strongly, enhancing durability yet complicating separation, whereas on aluminum it induces galvanic corrosion that weakens the base, and on copper it merges seamlessly, promoting heat transfer but risking delamination. Removal challenges arise from its persistent adhesion, influenced from environmental factors like humidity, yet laser cleaning proves effective, as the beam selectively ablates the nickel through precise vaporization, preserving underlying integrity without mechanical stress.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Multiple toxic compounds detected: Nickel, Phosphorus compounds (as phosphine), Nickel carbonyl - requires enhanced protection
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: Half-face or full-face respirator with organic vapor/particulate cartridges, adequate ventilation

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (15 ACH) for particulate control
